raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command to count total lines in all .gz files after decompression. Use zcat or gzip -dc. Find all .gz, decompress, count lines. Use cat? Use find and zcat | wc -l. Sum across all. So: find /testbed -type f -name '*.gz' -print0 | xargs -0 zcat | wc -l. That counts all lines. Output one line.
